body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: partial

## Reasoning

multi-turn-conversation chains three consecutive user prompts in ONE live session, asserting the parser resets state between turns (`ready → working → ready` once per turn) and that per-turn token contribution accumulates. It resolves to coverage cell `basic-turn` (aider: yes/partial), exercising the same turn-end-detection primitive (`headless_mode`) three times in one session rather than once. The load-bearing question is whether aider sustains a multi-turn REPL and whether the daemon flips back to `ready` between turns — not just at process exit.

**agent_supports: yes.** aider's REPL is natively multi-turn: one process holds the session open and appends every prompt + reply to the same per-cwd `.aider.chat.history.md`. `capabilities.json` records `tui: true`, and the committed `drive-aider-interactive.sh` drives that REPL under tmux with send/wait_turn steps — `wait_turn` blocks on each new `> Tokens: …` line before the next send, so three send+wait_turn pairs are one live session. The captured regression transcript (`regression/multi-turn-conversation/transcript.md`) shows all three turns (turn-one / turn-two / turn-three) with three distinct `> Tokens:` lines on a single `aider` invocation — direct proof aider holds one session across the three turns.

**irrlicht_observes: partial.** The aider adapter watches `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", Parser: RawLineParser}` — markdown, not JSONL. The parser opens a turn on each `#### ` user line and emits an `assistant_message` (carrying a per-call `Tokens` snapshot) at each `> Tokens:` line — but deliberately NOT a `turn_done` there, since under `--yes-always` aider may re-prompt the model within one turn. End-of-turn is therefore synthesized OUT-OF-BAND by `IdleFlush` only after the transcript is quiet for `aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter` (1500ms). The daemon's `SessionDetector` polls working sessions every `staleWorkingRefreshInterval` (5s), so the effective `working → ready` latency after a turn's last line is ~5s. `TestParser_MultiTurn_EmitsAssistantMessagePerTurn` and `TestParser_IdleFlush_AfterMultipleModelCalls_EmitsTurnDone` cover this two-stage shape.

The partial sits at the turn-boundary mechanism, not at observability of the arc itself: when each prompt is followed by ≥~5s idle (the canonical recipe's 3s sleep plus the wait_turn settle), IdleFlush fires and the daemon DOES observe three full `working → ready` arcs on one session, with `TotalTokens > 0` from the per-call snapshots. But the arc is fragile to cadence — `TestParser_NewUserPromptAfterMultiCall_StaysWorking` pins that a `####` arriving while the turn is still open (next prompt lands before IdleFlush) opens the new turn WITHOUT any intervening `turn_done`, so the session stays `working` across the boundary and the per-turn reset is lost. The captured regression events.jsonl shows exactly this failure mode: a single `ready → working` (seq 248) and no intermediate `working → ready` before the final `process_exited` (seq 469), because the driver's prompts outran the idle window. Combined with `cost_tracking: false` (no provider cost, only token counts), this keeps the verdict honest at `partial` rather than `yes`.

This ratifies the existing matrix row (aider/basic-turn: yes/partial, "idle-flush turn-end (~5s effective latency on working→ready)") — no drift.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: aider has no transcript turn-end marker; the per-turn working→ready flip is synthesized only by IdleFlush after ~1.5s quiet + the 5s detector poll (~5s effective latency). The three-turn arc is observable only when each prompt is followed by ≥~5s idle (canonical recipe's 3s sleep + wait_turn settle).

Turn boundaries blur under fast cadence: if the next #### prompt lands before IdleFlush fires, the parser opens the new turn with no intervening turn_done and the session stays working across the boundary (TestParser_NewUserPromptAfterMultiCall_StaysWorking). The captured regression run shows this — one ready→working and no per-turn ready flips before process_exited.

cost_tracking: false — per-turn Contribution carries token counts (sent/received) from each `> Tokens:` line but provider cost is absent unless aider prints a `, $… message` suffix; the spec's TotalTokens>0 assertion holds, dollar cost may not.
Exact assistant wording and per-turn token counts vary run-to-run (model-output variance); only the structural three-turn arc, single-session count, and final ready state are stable.

sources.note: FilesUnderCWD{Filename: .aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser}; CommandPattern /aider process match — markdown transcript, not JSONL.

Turn opens on `#### `; `> Tokens:` emits assistant_message (NOT turn_done) carrying a per-call Tokens snapshot; turn-end synthesized via IdleFlush after aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter (1500ms); comment documents ~5s effective working→ready latency.

TestParser_MultiTurn_EmitsAssistantMessagePerTurn (2 assistant_messages, no in-band turn_done); TestParser_IdleFlush_AfterMultipleModelCalls_EmitsTurnDone (idle synthesizes turn_done); TestParser_NewUserPromptAfterMultiCall_StaysWorking (fast next prompt → no per-turn reset).
staleWorkingRefreshInterval = 5 * time.Second — the working-session poll cadence that sets the ~5s effective working→ready latency for idle-flush turn-end.
tmux REPL driver; wait_turn blocks on a new `> Tokens:` line; keeps one aider session alive across all three send/wait_turn cycles — the basis for agent_supports: yes (native multi-turn REPL).
Captured real run: one aider invocation, three turns (turn-one/turn-two/turn-three) with three `> Tokens:` lines — proves aider holds one session across the three turns.
Captured daemon events: single ready→working (seq 248), no intermediate working→ready flips, terminal process_exited (seq 469) — direct evidence of the cadence-fragility caveat.
tui: true, headless_mode: true, streaming_output: true; cost_tracking: false, structured_output_jsonl: false (markdown transcript, no per-turn dollar cost).
